exemplar.title: How do different types of physical fatigue (aerobic vs. anaerobic) impact the reaction time of high school boys (age 14-18)?

detailedExemplarData.title: How do different types of physical fatigue (aerobic vs. anaerobic) impact the reaction time of high school boys (age 14-18)?

subject.title: Sports, exercise and health science (SEHS - Old)

strengths: Clear and sustained personal engagement through linking investigation to own basketball experience
Original design of questionnaires, consent forms and bespoke equipment protocol demonstrating initiative
Detailed rationale showing genuine curiosity and personal significance
feedback.calculationMethod: Holistic AI evaluation: All three strands are fulfilled at the highest level: the student consistently demonstrates independent thinking, personal input and clear personal significance throughout the investigation, matching the topâband descriptors.

strengths: Research question is highly focused, relevant and directly testable
Background information is entirely appropriate, wellâcited and scientifically coherent
Methodology is comprehensive and detailed, with clear HIIT and beepâtest protocols
Full awareness of safety and ethical issues demonstrated via robust risk assessments and consent procedures
weaknesses: Lacks randomization of session order to control learning or fatigueâorder effects
Hydration monitoring is assumed rather than explicitly measured
Hardware consistency (laptop and inputâdevice latency) could be better controlled
feedback.calculationMethod: Holistic AI evaluation: Despite minor methodological limitations, the investigation addresses all significant factors in design and implementation. The research question, background and safety planning are exemplary, justifying full marks for Exploration.

strengths: Raw data set is sufficiently large and relevant to support detailed conclusions
Data processing (averages, SDs, error bars, paired tâtests) is appropriate and clearly presented
Interpretation correctly links statistical findings back to physiological mechanisms
Awareness of measurement uncertainty is acknowledged (e.g., hardwareâdependence)
weaknesses: Uncertainty quantification is incomplete (device latencies not fully estimated)
Pâvalue significance threshold is not explicitly stated
feedback.calculationMethod: Holistic AI evaluation: The student demonstrates a strong level of analysis with accurate processing and valid interpretation of data. Some gaps in uncertainty quantification and statistical rigour prevent the maximum, but overall performance is in the top band.

strengths: Limitations are examined in depth, showing clear methodological insight
Suggestions for improvement are realistic, detailed and directly address identified issues
Critical reflection on data variability demonstrates solid evaluative thinking
weaknesses: Conclusion would benefit from explicit linkage of statistical results to claims
Comparison to broader scientific context is somewhat brief and general
Heartârate discussion lacks quantification against %HRmax to confirm fatigue levels
feedback.calculationMethod: Holistic AI evaluation: The evaluation provides a relevant conclusion with moderate support, strong discussion of limitations and excellent improvement suggestions. Some strand weaknesses in context comparison and statistical linkage limit it to the upper midâband.

strengths: Use of subjectâspecific terminology is appropriate and accurate
Report is well structured with logical flow and clear headings
Figures and tables (with error bars) effectively aid comprehension
weaknesses: Formatting glitches and cutâoff images disrupt readability in places
Repetitive phrasing of the research question reduces conciseness
Some figures lack inâaxis legends and embedded captions for selfâcontainment
feedback.calculationMethod: Holistic AI evaluation: Communication is generally clear and well organized with correct conventions and minimal errors. Minor presentation and formatting issues prevent the award of the maximum four marks.

strandSelections.reasoning: Uncertainty is considered via SD, acceptance range, and Â±0.5 % wheel accuracy, yet instrument/systematic uncertainties of laptops, mice and watches are not quantified.  Some consideration present but not comprehensiveâband 3.

The student relates findings to cited studies and physiological mechanisms, but discussion is brief and sometimes general.  Some correct contextualisation is evident, aligning with mid-band performance (3).
Sections follow IB IA conventions with logical flow from introduction to evaluation; appendices and tables are properly referenced.  Minor repetition but organisation is completeâscore 3.

Proposed improvements (standardised equipment, individualised fatigue thresholds, stricter anomaly criteria, larger sample, VOâmax grouping) are realistic, relevant and detailed, satisfying the highest descriptor.

The question is precise (type of fatigue, reaction time, defined age group) and directly testable, fulfilling the ârelevant and fully focusedâ criterion.  Minor wording repetition does not undermine focus, so a high score within the 5-6 band is justified.

Limitations are examined in a structured table, addressing fitness heterogeneity, equipment variance, rest delay, competitive environment and sample size, demonstrating strong methodological insightâwarrants high band score 5.

Methods are largely appropriate (beep test, HIIT protocol, repeated trials, controls) but notable weaknesses remain: equipment inconsistency, group testing, rest lag and unequal fatigue levels.  These limitations prevent a âhighly appropriateâ judgment, so mid-band 3 awarded.

Background covers CNS/PNS mechanisms, aerobic vs anaerobic physiology, previous findings and links them coherently to the RQ with citations.  Content is scientifically sound and wholly pertinent, matching the top-band description.
Most content is directly relevant; occasional lengthy background paragraphs and screenshot clutter slightly affect conciseness, yet understanding is not obscured.  Fits mid-high band.

Interpretation discusses overall trends, individual anomalies, overlapping error bars and t-test significance, linking back to physiology and hypothesis.  Some statements are tentative but generally correct and detailed, securing high end of 3-4 band (4).
Report is generally clear; figures, tables and headings aid comprehension although some formatting glitches exist (e.g., cut images).  Overall clarity meets 3-4 band baseline (score 3).

The student clearly links the study to personal basketball experience, designs bespoke forms, selects relevant equipment and independently organises participants.  The narrative shows sustained curiosity and ownership well beyond basic requirements, matching the top-band description.

Averages, standard deviations, error bars and paired t-tests are appropriate; calculations are shown.  However, outlier removal criteria are broad and some extreme values are kept, so accuracy/consistency is moderate, meriting upper-mid band (4).

The introduction explicitly explains why the issue matters to the student as a basketball player and to wider sporting contexts.  The rationale is specific (reaction-related mistakes in late quarters) and demonstrates genuine curiosity, meeting the highest descriptor.

Five trials per condition for ten participants provide ample relevant data.  Apart from a few missing heart-rate values, quantity and breadth are sufficient to support conclusions, fitting the 5-6 descriptor (score 5).

Original questionnaires, consent forms, risk tables and equipment sourcing all show initiative throughout design, implementation and presentation.  Decisions (e.g., online test instead of ruler drop, separate aerobic/anaerobic protocols) are clearly student-driven, warranting full marks for this strand.
A detailed risk table, warm-up procedures, hydration notes, consent forms and PAR-Q demonstrate full awareness of safety and ethical issues; no major omission identified, deserving high-band credit.
Subject terminology (e.g., ATP-PC, central vs peripheral fatigue, t-test) is used accurately with only minor stylistic errors.  Citation format is acceptable; hence a solid 3 within top band.

The conclusion directly answers the RQ, references data trends and acknowledges variability, providing moderate support.  Depth is good but weakened by data reliability concerns, so 3-4 band top score (4).

annotations.comment: Methodology lacks randomization of test order; randomise session order to counter learning or fatigueâorder effects.
Anaerobic protocol is clearly described in discrete steps, ensuring reproducibility of intenseâinterval fatigue induction.
Risk assessment table identifies hazards and controls comprehensively, demonstrating full safety awareness.
Hydration is assumed rather than tracked; explicitly instruct participants to drink measured volumes and record intake.
The introduction effectively links the inquiry to the studentâs personal basketball experience, demonstrating clear personal engagement and ownership of the investigation.
The research question is focused, precise and directly testable, specifying fatigue types, reaction time and age group.
Avoid repeating the research question verbatim; summarise in your own words to maintain reader engagement and reduce redundancy.
Background is thorough, covering CNS/PNS mechanisms, muscle fatigue types and relevant literature with citations.
The aerobicâfatigue paragraph ends abruptly; expand to fully explain how oxygen consumption and neuronal function interact under fatigue.
Hypothesis is concise and physiologically justified, making a clear prediction for the comparison of anaerobic vs. aerobic fatigue.
Controlledâvariable table notes identical laptops but does not address processing speed differences; standardise hardware to reduce systematic error.
Citation of OpenAI (2024) in background lacks scholarly peerâreviewed support; replace with primary literature to strengthen scientific context.
Online reaction test reduces human error compared to rulerâdrop; this control measure improves data accuracy.
Uncontrolledâvariables list lacks prioritisation of highâimpact factors, making mitigation planning diffuse; highlight the most influential and address them first.
Sampling via purposive selection of recreational cross-country athletes justifies homogeneity and reduces fitnessâlevel variability.
Apparatus table omits uncertainty estimates for laptops, mice and smartwatches; quantify inputâdevice latency and sensor error.
Acknowledging the reactionâtime testâs hardware dependence shows awareness of measurement uncertainty.
Figures of setup lack dimension annotations; include scale bars or labels to clarify spatial relationships for the reader.
Ethical section clearly states informed consent, right to withdraw and PAR-Q screening, showing robust ethical awareness.
Include axis labels and legends in figures rather than external captions to improve accessibility and selfâcontainment.
Interpretation critically reflects on overlapping error bars and data variability, demonstrating sound evaluative insight.
Anomaly threshold of Â±150 ms seems arbitrary; justify cutoff statistically (e.g., based on percentile ranges) to improve rigour.
Processed data table provides averages and standard deviations per participant, supporting detailed analysis.
Spreadsheet formula for t-test is shown but lacks explanation of tail and type parameters; clarify these choices in the write-up.
P-values are reported without specifying significance threshold (e.g., Î± = 0.05); state criteria for statistical significance.
Graph of individual data is clear with error bars; visual presentation aids interpretation of variability.
Heartârate discussion lacks quantification against maximum heart rate; record %HRmax to confirm that participants reached intended fatigue levels.
Conclusion acknowledges hypothesis validation and data limitations but would benefit from explicit linkage of statistical results to claims.
Future research section proposes realistic and relevant investigations that extend and deepen the current study.
